name: Penguin
summary: Penguins are flightless seabirds built for swimming in cold southern oceans. They waddle on land, dive for fish and krill, and many species raise chicks together in huge noisy colonies.
metaTitle: Penguin Facts | Habitat, Diet, Speed & Photos
metaDescription: Penguin facts with photos, habitat, diet, colony life, swimming speed, and conservation. Learn where penguins live in the Southern Hemisphere.

lifespan.captivity: Up to 30 years in human care

speed: Up to 22 mph while swimming underwater
adaptations: thick fat layer in cold species

reproduction.gestationOrIncubation: About 1 to 2 months of egg incubation

funFacts: Penguins cannot fly, but they swim as well as many fish.
Emperor penguins breed during the harsh Antarctic winter.
A group of penguins in the water is called a raft.
Penguins have special glands that remove salt from seawater.
Some penguin colonies hold tens of thousands of nesting birds.
Penguins leap out of the water onto ice in a move called porpoising.

faq.question: What is a penguin?
faq.answer: A penguin is a flightless seabird that lives in the Southern Hemisphere. Penguins have flipper-like wings, waterproof feathers, and strong legs for walking on land and ice.

faq.question: Can penguins fly?
faq.answer: No. Penguins lost the ability to fly long ago. Their wings became stiff flippers that push them fast through ocean water instead.

faq.question: Where do penguins live?
faq.answer: Almost all penguins live in the Southern Hemisphere, from Antarctica to South America, Africa, Australia, and nearby islands. The Galápagos penguin lives near the equator.

faq.question: What do penguins eat?
faq.answer: Penguins are carnivores that eat fish, krill, and squid. They catch prey by diving and chasing underwater.

faq.question: How fast can penguins swim?
faq.answer: Many penguins swim about 4 to 7 mph during normal travel. In short bursts, some species can reach around 22 mph underwater.

faq.question: Do penguins live in groups?
faq.answer: Yes. Most penguin species nest in large colonies where thousands of birds may gather. Parents take turns guarding chicks and finding food.

faq.question: What is a baby penguin called?
faq.answer: A baby penguin is called a chick. Chicks hatch from eggs and depend on their parents for warmth and food.

faq.question: Why are penguins black and white?
faq.answer: Their dark backs blend with deep water when seen from above. Their white fronts match bright sky when seen from below, helping them hide from predators and prey.

faq.question: Are penguins endangered?
faq.answer: It depends on the species. Some, like the emperor penguin, are Near Threatened. Others, like the African penguin, are Endangered because of fishing, pollution, and habitat loss.

faq.question: How do penguins stay warm in Antarctica?
faq.answer: Emperor penguins have thick feathers and a layer of fat. They also huddle together in tight groups and take turns standing in the warm center of the huddle.
